skill.name: python-fastapi
skill.description: Guides FastAPI API development with uv, modular project structure, async SQLAlchemy, Alembic, testing, and production-oriented patterns.
skill.summary: Build structured FastAPI services with uv, async SQLAlchemy, migrations, tests, and typed application patterns.
skill.author: 0xkynz

security_audit.summary: AI review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, obfuscation, or credential exfiltration in the single markdown skill file. Most static findings are false positives from fenced examples, but the guide contains copy-paste risks including curl piped to sh, permissive CORS settings, placeholder secrets, and 0.0.0.0 server bindings.

medium_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped to Shell
medium_findings.description: The setup guide tells users to install uv by piping a remote HTTPS script directly into sh. This is common tooling guidance, but it executes network content without local review and creates supply-chain risk if copied blindly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is present in a bash example and directly pipes downloaded content into a shell. It appears intended for legitimate uv installation, so the risk is guidance-related rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Permissive CORS Example With Credentials
medium_findings.description: The FastAPI example enables wildcard origins, methods, and headers while allowing credentials. A comment says to configure this for production, but copying the example unchanged can create unsafe browser access policy.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The insecure CORS settings are visible in application example code. The nearby comment lowers confidence of harmful intent but does not remove the copy-paste risk.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Secrets and Database URL in Examples
low_findings.description: The configuration example includes a sample database URL with user and password fields and a default secret key. These values look like placeholders, but production users must replace them with real secret management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are clearly template defaults and the document later says never to commit secrets. The risk is low because no real credential or exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives From Markdown Code Blocks
low_findings.description: Most external command, dynamic import, SQLite, weak crypto, and reconnaissance alerts came from fenced markdown examples or ordinary Python imports. I did not find prompt injection, obfuscation, credential exfiltration, or hidden executable files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a single markdown skill containing instructional snippets. The flagged lines are examples, not code that runs automatically when the skill is loaded.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe to Shell Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The pattern curl ... | sh is present in setup instructions. This should be replaced or accompanied by checksum verification and a manual install alternative.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is an exact pipe-to-shell command in a bash block. The source is a legitimate package manager URL, but the execution pattern remains risky.

dangerous_patterns.title: Development Server Bound to All Interfaces
dangerous_patterns.description: The run commands bind uvicorn to 0.0.0.0. This is useful for containers but can expose a development server on reachable network interfaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and could expose local services if copied. This is common deployment guidance, so it is a moderate operational warning rather than evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent FastAPI structure for APIs, database access, migrations, and tests. This skill provides practical patterns for uv, async SQLAlchemy, Pydantic, Alembic, and production setup.
actual_capabilities: Defines a feature-based FastAPI project structure with app, feature, API, model, service, and repository modules.
Provides uv setup commands for dependencies, development tools, Python version pinning, and project initialization.
Shows Pydantic settings, async SQLAlchemy engine setup, session dependency handling, and Alembic migration patterns.
Demonstrates user feature schemas, models, repositories, services, routers, and response models.
Includes pytest and httpx examples for async FastAPI endpoint testing with dependency overrides.
Lists common development commands for running, testing, linting, formatting, and type checking the service.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include a complete runnable repository.
Authentication utilities are referenced but not fully implemented in the document.
Production security settings require review before copying examples into a deployed service.
The examples focus on PostgreSQL and SQLite, not every database or deployment platform.

use_cases.title: Start a New API Service
use_cases.description: Create a structured FastAPI project with uv dependencies, typed settings, async database access, and basic routes.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Patterns
use_cases.description: Align feature modules, repositories, services, schemas, routers, migrations, and tests across Python API projects.

use_cases.title: Add Database and Test Scaffolding
use_cases.description: Introduce async SQLAlchemy, Alembic migrations, pytest fixtures, and httpx client tests to an existing FastAPI app.

prompt_templates.title: Create a FastAPI Starter
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI project outline using this skill. Include uv setup, package choices, folder structure, and the first health endpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Feature Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a products feature with schemas, model, repository, service, router, and tests for list and create operations.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Existing App
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FastAPI project against this skill. Identify structure gaps, database issues, test gaps, and production configuration risks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this skill to prepare a FastAPI service for production. Cover settings, migrations, CORS, database pooling, testing, linting, and operational risks.

output_examples.input: Design a new FastAPI user feature.
output: A module layout for users with API, schema, model, repository, and service files.
A description of request and response models, database fields, and service responsibilities.
A focused test plan for create, read, update, duplicate email, and not found cases.
A gap list comparing the project to feature-based architecture.
Recommended changes for settings, database sessions, migrations, and router aggregation.
Security notes for secrets, CORS, public bind addresses, and production defaults.
An async testing approach using pytest, httpx, dependency overrides, and isolated database sessions.
Suggested unit and integration test boundaries for API routes and service logic.

best_practices: Replace placeholder secrets, database credentials, CORS origins, and host bindings before production use.
Keep each feature module self-contained with schemas, models, services, repositories, APIs, and tests.
Run formatting, linting, type checks, tests, and migrations as part of every delivery workflow.
anti_patterns: Do not copy development CORS settings into production without explicit allowed origins.
Do not commit .env files, secret keys, database passwords, tokens, or generated credentials.
Do not run remote install scripts or migration commands without reviewing their source and target environment.

faq.question: Does this skill create a complete FastAPI application?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns, examples, and setup guidance that an AI assistant can apply to your project.

faq.question: Which package manager does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends uv for project setup, dependency installation, Python version pinning, and running development commands.

faq.question: Does it support async database access?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use SQLAlchemy async sessions, asyncpg for PostgreSQL, and Alembic migration guidance.

faq.question: Is the included configuration production ready?
faq.answer: Not by itself. You must replace placeholder secrets, configure CORS, and review host binding and database settings.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools for applying the skill guidance.

faq.question: What security review result should publishers note?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it has medium copy-paste risks from shell install guidance and development defaults.
